codeReview.summary: The reviewed handoff claims the RecordId roundtrip table-identity fix was already present in the baseline, but the cited feature-owned commit 1dc940c does not implement that behavior. Its diff only adopts typed decode-error classification, while the actual requested get_record reinjection seam appears later in repository code and was separately revalidated through later evidence artifacts.

issues.description: The feature handoff attributes the RecordId table-identity fix to commit 1dc940c, but that commit's diff does not touch Repo::get_record or inject the caller-provided RecordId back into decode_stored_row_value. The claimed implementation/proof ownership is therefore incorrect for this feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker procedure does not clearly distinguish evidence-only verification from implementation work, so workers keep marking implementation features successful against an already-landed baseline without producing a feature-owned commit or explicit evidence chain.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker session e90d3625-0621-4a50-9ebd-0e082a74a2e4 reported success with commit 1dc940c and no code edits even though that commit diff only covers typed decode classification. A later evidence-revalidation artifact was needed to point this feature at the actual implementation/proof commits.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission review flow allowed a feature review file to remain tied to the wrong commit after the validator had already identified ownership/evidence problems, which suggests the expected repair path for mis-owned commits is not clearly encoded in mission conventions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The prior review at .factory/validation/persistence-primitive-and-errors/scrutiny/reviews/milestone-d-record-id-roundtrip-table-identity-fix-evidence-revalidation.json passes only after re-linking the behavior to commit f6cbcd5 and proof commit 4aec035, while the original feature handoff still points at 1dc940c.

summary: Fail: the reviewed feature report is still tied to commit 1dc940c, but that commit does not implement the requested Repo::get_record RecordId table-identity fix. The actual behavior was established by later commits/evidence, so this feature's ownership remains incorrect.
